text: Not Scatha. Not a divine worm manifesting from another dimension.

The irrigation channels in the earth begin to fill — slowly — from somewhere underground. Water seeping up through the old system, disturbed by the weight of five people standing in the rings. Ancient pipes doing what they were built to do.
And in the water filling the rings, in the mica-chalk Scathagram becoming a mirror for the moon—
Worms. Many worms. Coming up through the wet earth in the concentric rings, gathering at the edges of the chalk circle, doing what worms do when the ground gets wet.

speaker: Grubwin
text: That's — that's just because of the moisture—
Grubwin looks. The moonlight. The water in the channels. The worms at the edge of the circle, dozens of them, the mica-chalk gleaming. Twenty poems in a stack in Vermia's hands.
